description.purpose: diagnostic_uncertainty — 47-year-old female with I've been having visual changes or spots in your vision for a few weeks
description.clinical_scenario: Patient presents with I've been having visual changes or spots in your vision for a few weeks. Requires history taking, examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning.
educational_objectives: Ability to probe for hidden symptoms
description.focus: Systematic information gathering and differential diagnosis

user_scenario.persona: 47-year-old female patient, 小学 education, works as a factory worker. a patient who may forget to mention some symptoms presenting with I've been having visual changes or spots in your vision. Accompanied by daughter (age 27).

patient_profile.chief_complaint: kidney disease for a few weeks

patient_knows.general_concern: I've been having visual changes or spots in your vision

medication_concerns: I feel fine, do I really need to take blood pressure medicine?
Will I become dependent on blood pressure medication?
I heard blood pressure medicine causes impotence
lifestyle_beliefs: My blood pressure is only high because I'm stressed at work
If I cut out salt completely, I won't need medication
economic_concerns: How much will this medication cost per month?
Can we only prescribe what's absolutely necessary?

share_only_if_asked: high blood pressure (needs doctor to specifically ask)
high blood pressure (needs doctor to specifically ask)
hidden_truth: long-term renal failure (won't mention unless specifically probed)
high blood pressure (won't mention unless specifically probed)
high blood pressure (won't mention unless specifically probed)
information_sharing_strategy.hidden_note: Only revealed through careful, specific questioning by the doctor
misleading_symptoms: visual changes or spots in vision
morning headache (also seen in sleep apnea and migraine)
dizziness (also seen in anemia and inner ear disorders)
information_sharing_strategy.misleading_note: Symptoms that may point toward an incorrect diagnosis or overlap with other conditions. Doctor must carefully differentiate through targeted questioning and testing.

progressive_reveal.trigger: doctor asks a specific follow-up question about related symptoms
doctor asks a specific follow-up question about related symptoms

share_in_response_to_medication_discussi: Concerns about lisinopril side effects — pregnancy
family_member_will_interject: daughter: How can we help at home?
daughter: What should we cook for them?

user_scenario.task_instructions: You are HuangXX, a 47-year-old female patient. Your education level is 小学. You work as a factory worker. You are seeing a doctor because of kidney disease. This has been going on for about a few weeks. Answer honestly but you may forget to mention some symptoms. If the doctor doesn't specifically ask about something, you might not remember it. Your memory for details is not great — approximate answers are fine. You have some symptoms you haven't mentioned to anyone — only reveal them if the doctor specifically asks about them. You are sensitive about medical costs. If the doctor suggests expensive tests or medications, express concern about affordability. Your daughter is with you and may speak up during the consultation to ask questions or express concerns.

family_member_scenario.scenario: daughter accompanies patient, participates in the entire visit
family_member_scenario.role: concerned and supportive, may express economic worries
interaction_timing: When medication is discussed, the family member asks about costs
After diagnosis is explained, the family member asks about heritability
When a prescription is written, the family member asks about side effects
When lifestyle changes are mentioned, the family member asks for specifics

communication_checks.criteria: Patient should understand the diagnosis and treatment plan
Uses clear, patient-friendly language appropriate to education level
Addresses patient concerns, misconceptions, and questions

chief_complaint_detail.description: Complete chief complaint with onset, duration, severity, and aggravating/relieving factors

symptoms_completeness.description: All major symptoms of hypertension identified through systematic questioning

past_medical_history.description: Relevant past medical history including comorbidities

current_medications.description: Current medication list with doses and adherence

must_ask: Do you get headaches, especially in the morning?
Have you noticed any visual changes or spots in your vision?
Do you experience palpitations or racing heartbeat?

treatment_scenarios.description: Patient reports side effects from lisinopril (ACE inhibitor)
options: Add adjunct medication to manage side effects
treatment_scenarios.correct_approach: Start with low-dose lisinopril, titrate up, monitor symptoms; consider alternative if intolerant

drug_interaction_check.description: Must check drug interactions before prescribing

bleeding_risk_with_anticoagulants.description: Must assess: bleeding risk with anticoagulants

blood_pressure_control.description: Must assess and address blood pressure

forbidden_actions: Agree with patient misconceptions without correction
Ignore abnormal lab values without explanation or follow-up plan
Diagnose without asking about symptom onset, duration, and progression
Diagnose without exploring at least one differential diagnosis
Dismiss patient concerns without addressing them
Use medical jargon without explanation when patient has limited health literacy
Fail to correct patient misconceptions about treatment
Prescribe contraindicated drug without documenting rationale
Order unnecessary tests without clinical justification
Prescribe NSAIDs without assessing cardiovascular risk
Fail to consider Secondary Hypertension as alternative diagnosis
Prescribe lisinopril in patient with pregnancy without special precautions
Diagnose hypertension without ordering any supporting lab tests

uncertainty_events.event: Lab results show inconsistent or borderline findings
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Order repeat/confirmatory test, consider pre-analytical error, correlate with clinical symptoms before concluding

uncertainty_events.event: Patient casually mentions 'chronic kidney disease' when discussing treatment options
uncertainty_events.example: 'Oh, I also sometimes get chronic kidney disease — is that related?'
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Recognize this may indicate undiagnosed kidney disease, update differential, order appropriate workup
